top_categories.workout_context: 13,123ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
156ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Granfondo Gavia e Mortirolo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Gavia e Mortirolo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Granfondo Gavia e Mortirolo's paved Italian Alpine mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 84 miles.
13,123ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Gavia e Mortirolo. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 84, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 84 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Low-cadence force work for 13,123ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo Gavia e Mortirolo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Italian Alpine mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Granfondo Gavia e Mortirolo’s 84 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Granfondo Gavia e Mortirolo with 13,123ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Gavia summit at 2,618m: temperatures can drop to 2-5C with wind chill below zero; 25C+ temperature swing between valley and Gavia summit in a single ride.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Granfondo Gavia e Mortirolo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved italian alpine mountain passes to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 84 miles of paved italian alpine mountain passes in Ponte di Legno, Brescia, Lombardy, Italy.
